target_hints: Waits for the example root to exist.
Toggles Pixelate/Blur/None and captures screenshots + bundle.
Waits for the hello example root to exist.
Captures a baseline screenshot + bundle.
Waits for the counter view to exist.
Clicks increment and captures a screenshot + bundle.
Opens the dialog via the trigger.
Clicks the close affordance.
Waits for the content node to disappear.
Captures a screenshot + bundle.
Clicks the dialog trigger.
Waits for the content node to exist.
Captures a screenshot + bundle.
Waits for the example root to exist.
Captures Light and Dark screenshots + bundle.
Waits for the example root to exist.
Toggles the panel via shortcut.
Disables availability and asserts the shortcut no longer toggles.
Types into the input and asserts length via numeric semantics.
Presses Enter to submit and asserts the submit counter increments.
Presses Escape to clear and asserts the length returns to 0.
Asserts the default dock graph shape for the docking arbitration demo (dockview-style).
This gate intentionally does not include split fractions/shares.
Drag a docked tab to the float-zone affordance (top-right of the dock tab bar) and drop it.
Expected behavior: the panel becomes an in-window floating container (no OS window tear-off).
This gate is invariant-based (dock diagnostics), not pixel-based.
Injects an IncomingOpenRequest event and asserts it is observable via event_kind_seen.
This gates that the app driver records platform-delivered events into the diagnostics ring buffer.
Opens a popover and asserts docking drag closes it (capture), then opens a dialog and asserts underlay click does not leak through.
This script exercises an opt-in behavior: creating multiple DockFloating OS windows without merging the first one back.
It tears off two distinct tabs (left + right) from the main window, then drags them back one-by-one and asserts the dock graph returns to the canonical signature.
Note: cursor overrides are expressed in window-client logical pixels.
This script exercises a cross-window tab drag: tear off a tab to create a floating OS window, overlap the windows, then drag the tab back into the main window.
It relies on schema v2 window-targeted steps to migrate the active script across windows.
Note: cursor overrides are expressed in window-client logical pixels.
This script overlaps two OS windows and asserts that cross-window dock drag hover routing follows window z-order when the cursor is in the overlap region.
It raises each window without moving the cursor and expects `dock_drag_current_window_is` to switch accordingly (ImGui-style).
Cursor overrides use window-client logical coordinates to stay stable under DPI scaling.
This script gates a Windows-specific fallback: finishing a cross-window dock drag when the left mouse release is not delivered to any window.
It starts a cross-window tab drag, intentionally omits pointer_up, then uses a diagnostics-only mouse-button override to make the runner believe the left button is down while the OS reports it as up.
The runner should detect this mismatch and finish the drag using cursor-based drop routing (ImGui-style).
This script repeatedly tears off a tab to create a floating OS window, then drags it back into the main window.
Expected behavior: after each cycle the dock graph returns to the default canonical signature without deepening splits or growing node count.
Note: cursor overrides are expressed in window-client physical pixels.
This script starts a tab drag, asserts the docking preview becomes active, then presses Escape mid-drag and asserts the drag session is no longer active.
This script drags the splitter far beyond the window bounds in both directions.
Expected behavior: the adjacent viewport panels clamp at the docking policy minimum size and never collapse.
Run with env: FRET_DOCK_ARB_PRESET=large
Optional: FRET_DOCK_ARB_NO_PERSIST=1 to avoid writing .fret/layout.json
Run with env: FRET_DOCK_ARB_PRESET=large
Recommended: FRET_DOCK_ARB_DISALLOW_DROP_TARGETS=1 (avoid layout mutation on drop)
Optional: FRET_DOCK_ARB_NO_PERSIST=1 to avoid writing .fret/layout.json
Builds a long tab stack by merging multiple single-tab viewports into one leaf, then asserts drop-at-end resolves insert_index == tab_count.
This gate avoids pixel-based tab rect assumptions; it relies on docking interaction diagnostics.
This script first merges two viewports into a single tab stack, then asserts drop-at-end resolves insert_index == 2.
It cancels the final drag (Escape) to avoid mutating layout beyond the setup step.
Cycles the built-in node graph preset families via shortcut (paint-only skin revision).
Starts a wire drag session and captures bundles for debugging skin-driven chrome.
Toggles wire glow so bundles capture both glow and no-glow variants.
Toggles wire highlight so bundles capture both highlight and no-highlight variants.
Toggles edge markers so bundles capture both markers and no-markers variants.
Exercises invalid + convertible drops without mutating the graph (conversion picker forced).
Sets theme preset to zinc/light then zinc/dark.
Navigates to the Button Group page.
Captures screenshots for the Size section (icon-only Add button should not be clipped).
Requires the UI gallery Carousel page to include a `Loop` section with `loop=true` + Embla engine enabled.
Expected behavior: repeated touch swipes do not clamp at ends or hang; content remains visible.
Sets OS clipboard text and pastes into a focused textarea to validate the end-to-end paste flow deterministically.
Note: on native runners this mutates the OS clipboard (not a sandboxed clipboard).
Exercises the intent-level long_press step (touch-first).
Fails fast when the runner does not advertise diag.gesture_long_press.
Exercises the intent-level pinch step (touch-first).
Waits for a pinch_gesture event in the diagnostics ring as proof of delivery.
Fails fast when the runner does not advertise diag.gesture_pinch.
Exercises the intent-level swipe step (touch-first).
Waits for pointer.up in the diagnostics ring as proof of delivery.
Fails fast when the runner does not advertise diag.gesture_swipe.
Exercises the intent-level tap step (touch-first).
Fails fast when the runner does not advertise diag.gesture_tap.
Exercises pen pointer injection via pointer_kind=pen.
Fails fast when the runner does not advertise diag.pointer_kind_pen.
Exercises touch pointer injection via pointer_kind=touch.
Fails fast when the runner does not advertise diag.pointer_kind_touch.
fret-ui-gallery
fret-ui-gallery
Waits for the close button on the active tab (pane A, doc-a-2).
Clicks the close button.
Asserts the tab is removed from the UI tree.
Activates doc-a-1 (pane A) so MRU is deterministic.
Closes doc-a-1 via the close button.
Asserts doc-a-1 is removed and selection falls back to doc-a-2 (MRU fallback).
Drags a tab from pane A toward the right edge of pane B.
Waits for the right-edge drop preview indicator to appear.
Captures a screenshot while the preview is visible (used to gate docking drop overlay tokens).
Waits for a known tab in pane A to exist.
Middle-clicks the tab.
Asserts the tab is removed from the UI tree (not_exists).
Shrinks the window to force tab strip overflow.
Opens the overflow menu.
Selects a known tab entry from the overflow list.
Asserts the tab becomes selected.
Shrinks the window to force tab strip overflow.
Drags the first tab onto the explicit end-drop surface.
Asserts the tab becomes the last tab (pos_in_set == set_size).
Asserts initial pos_in_set for a known tab.
Drags the first tab to the end and drops it.
Asserts pos_in_set updates after the reorder.
